meta.title: A2UI expert harness — reconciled scope: 2 skills + composer/reviewer agent pair · 3 rubrics (.claude/docs/rubrics/) · judge activation (corpus ADR-0060 seam) · procedural compose-verify loop + validate CLI · wiring governance
meta.author: planner (design seat — the expert-harness intake, NEXT item 1)
upstream: .claude/docs/specs/specs/a2ui-training-corpus.spec.md (v0.5 — SPEC-R8 tier-2 bar; R3 AC2 calibration)
.claude/docs/specs/llds/a2ui-corpus-store.lld.md (v0.5.1 — the realized substrate; the C12 split; §0 deferred tail C8)
.claude/docs/adr/0060-corpus-store-phase1-admission-seams.md (accepted — the judge seam this wave activates)
meta.seat: authoring slices h3-h9 (rubrics/skills/agents): planner-or-host via rubric-author/skill-author/agent-author, graded by the global doc-reviewer/skill-reviewer/agent-reviewer seats; PACKAGE-CODE slices h10/h11: a2ui-builder; activation h12 + reference-artifact h15: host-coordinated with a2ui-reviewer as the critic; h13 routing corpora + h14 wiring script: planner/host. Gates per slice named in each accept.
meta.deferred: a2ui-jsonl-mcp skill (trigger: the streaming producer wave, NEXT item 2) · programmatic loop driver (trigger: the live-agent wave, NEXT item 4 — streaming LLD-C2's dependency re-pointed there, edit APPLIED this intake) · retrieval CLI (trigger: live-agent wave or corpus >20 records) · corpus LLD-C8 contamination + the first eval set + LLD-C12's Inspect-AI scoring/lift leg (trigger: the first eval record; upstream eval/a2ui_eval/{scorers,dataset}.py fetches escalated for THAT wave) · wiring-check promotion to a standing gate (trigger: the first observed wiring-drift incident)
meta.diff: v1 → v2 (2026-07-03, the independent doc-review's revision pass — 1 blocker + 4 majors + minors, architecture unchanged): meta.seat slice numbers CORRECTED (v1 mis-said h8/h9 = builder and h10/h13 = activation; it is h10/h11 = a2ui-builder, h12 = activation, h15 = reference artifact — M2); h11's accept absorbs the B1 gate amendment (corpus-data.test.ts:45's never-quarantined assertion is amended in the SAME slice that makes quarantine reachable, its facet-only invariant-ii cite corrected) and the M1 quarantine-survivability guards (store.all includeQuarantined warming · quarantined-name import halt · --replace judged exit · rescore all-or-nothing/halt-on-unknown-or-conflict/one-way semantics — ADR-0068 cl.4-6); h12's accept expects the AMENDED gate and names the plant/revert procedure (temporary shelf seed, exits 1, nothing written, reverted); h15's accept rephrased per M3 (host-orchestrated rounds; the composer never SELF-GRADES but receives the critic's verdict between rounds); h3's [gate] evidence = the validate-payload CLI (not the shard-scoped corpus-data gate); h5 adds the explicit rubric version: marker the VerdictsFile cites; h8 adds the a2ui-builder stale rubrics-pending line repair; h14 states a2ui-builder is OUT of the maker→graded-by enumeration; h6's label drops the ambiguous R1a+R1b lettering (v0.2 re-lettered); upstream cites bumped to corpus LLD v0.5.1 / harness LLD v0.2.1. Nodes/actions/hosts/edges are otherwise IDENTICAL to v1. IN-PLACE ACCEPT REFINEMENT (2026-07-03, the PROMOTE micro-pass — sanctioned on this UNDISPATCHED manifest, no structural change): h11's accept gains (a) the parseVerdictsFile(text, expectedRubricVersion) signature (caller supplies the version; the pure core never reads the rubric doc), (b) the rescore inverse clause (records not named in the file → untouched, reported still-unjudged), and (c) the --replace dedup self-collision rule (predecessor's exact+near signatures omitted from warming for that run) with the NEAR-IDENTICAL fixture requirement; the quarantined-name halt tightened to 'clears dedup whose name matches'.

nodes.label: Expert-harness wave (harness SPEC v0.2 / LLD v0.2.1)
harness SPEC v0.2 + LLD v0.2.1 reconciliation (done in this intake, incl. the review revision pass)
nodes.accept: a2ui-expert-harness.spec.md at v0.2 and a2ui-harness-wiring.lld.md at v0.2.1: every v0.1 SPEC-R#/LLD-C# carries a REALIZED-ELSEWHERE (file:line cite) / STILL-NEEDED (reshaped how) / OBSOLETE (why) ruling; rubric home corrected to .claude/docs/rubrics/; SPEC-N2 re-bound to the realized routing_eval.py tripwire + human-read policy; the loop contract split procedural-now / programmatic-at-live-agent with rounds HOST-orchestrated (LLD §6); the streaming LLD-C2 re-point APPLIED (a2ui-streaming-pipeline.lld.md §1 row + §3 lead); harness_checks.py spec + lld exit 0 on the two docs; all cross-references resolve

nodes.label: ADR-0067 (harness right-sizing) + ADR-0068 (judge verdict-adapter + quarantine contract) PROPOSED → ratified
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0 on 0067 and 0068; both carry Status: proposed (never self-ratified) with index rows in adr/README.md; ADR-0068's Repairs names the corpus LLD §0/§1/§11 C12-split edit, the corpus-data.test.ts gate amendment, and the store.ts includeQuarantined flag; ADR-0067's Repairs names the applied streaming-LLD re-point; build slices h3-h11 dispatch only after the host accepts the ADR each encodes

nodes.label: rubric .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-payload.md
nodes.accept: python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-author/scripts/harness_checks.py rubric exits 0 on the file; every dimension typed [gate]/[review] with 1/3/5 anchors and a what-it-checks column; gate-to-promote rule states every [gate] dim hard + gated dims >= 4; [gate] dims CITE the validate-payload CLI's verdicts as their evidence (the payload-scoped probe — NOT the shard-scoped corpus-data gate) rather than re-judging them (process.md rule 1 — no dimension owns a true/false a script already decides); [review] dims cover composition/idiom (against the seed shelf + catalog docs), binding hygiene, accessibility intent; doc-reviewer (fresh context) scores >= 4 on rubric-author's references/rubric.md gated dims

nodes.label: rubric .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-catalog.md
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py rubric exits 0; [gate] dims cite the REALIZED naming/conformance probes (src/catalog/naming.ts UAX-31 + conformance.ts) as their evidence; [review] dims cover mapping fidelity to ui-* controls, PropDef typing idiom, example/doc coverage per row; gate-to-promote present; anchors cite a realized catalog row (the form-family rows, ADR-0053) as the reference artifact; doc-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: rubric .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-corpus.md (the tier-2 judge standard)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py rubric exits 0; dimensions cover ground-truth validity (CITES a2ui-payload.md for the a2uiOutput leg — no duplicated dimensions), prompt/description quality, target-clarity (the target ?? description consumer rule, ADR-0063), provenance integrity, dedup adjacency; defines the SPEC-R8 aggregation the Judge seam reads: qualityScore = MIN across [gate]-typed dims on the 1-5 scale, passed = qualityScore >= 4; carries an EXPLICIT version: marker that every VerdictsFile must cite as rubricVersion (ADR-0068 cl.1 / SPEC §5.3); CALIBRATION record committed in the rubric (SPEC-R3 AC2): the same reference record scored twice in independent fresh contexts agrees within +/-1 on every gated dim, tolerance stated; doc-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: skill .claude/skills/a2ui-compose/ (merged the v0.1 patterns+composition areas)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py skill exits 0 on SKILL.md (trigger in first ~200 chars; <= 500 lines; validation loop present); description fences siblings (NOT for ui-* control code [component-author], a2ui package source [a2ui-builder], corpus curation [a2ui-corpus-curate], site pages [docs-author]); body encodes the SPEC-R6 bounded loop — compose, run the validate-payload CLI, self-correct, maxRounds 3 then halt-and-report, with rounds HOST-orchestrated and rubric grading the CRITIC's not the composer's (harness LLD §6 round-orchestration rules); references/ carries per-type idiom cards DERIVED from the realized catalog + the 11-seed shelf (file cites, no restated protocol.ts facts); skill-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: skill .claude/skills/a2ui-corpus-curate/ (thin pointer over the realized pipeline)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py skill exits 0; the body is a THIN procedure over the REALIZED pipeline — author seed (src/examples shape, ADR-0055) -> import-seeds --verdicts -> gates (the AMENDED corpus-data.test.ts) -> judge verdicts (a2ui-reviewer against a2ui-corpus.md) -> rescore for back-scoring, --replace for the judged quarantine exit — citing corpus LLD §6 + harness LLD §7 + ADR-0055/0060..0064/0068 rather than restating them; states the THREE halt escalations (theta_dup near-dup between distinct seeds; unjudged candidate under a wired judge; quarantined-name collision); NO restated record schema (record.ts owns it); skill-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: agents .claude/agents/a2ui-composer.md + the a2ui-builder.md routing repair
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py agent exits 0 on a2ui-composer.md; frontmatter: scoped tools (Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Bash — Bash justified solely for the validate-payload CLI), model+effort set, maker trigger description with sibling fence; body names 'graded by: a2ui-payload rubric (.claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-payload.md)' and NEVER self-grades (SPEC-R8 — it receives the critic's verdict between host-orchestrated rounds but never assigns its own scores); charter encodes corpus conditioning (read corpus/exemplar/v1_0/agent-ui.jsonl + the examples pages directly at <= ~20 records; the retrieval CLI is the NAMED scale trigger, not built) and the SPEC-R6 loop with the CLI as the deterministic check; a2ui-builder.md repaired in the same slice: (a) payload COMPOSITION routes to a2ui-composer, package source/renderer/catalog CODE stays a2ui-builder (both descriptions fence each other) and (b) its stale 'the a2ui-specific rubrics land with the expert harness — pending' line updated to cite the landed .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-*.md set; agent-reviewer >= 4 gated on both files

nodes.label: agent .claude/agents/a2ui-reviewer.md (the a2ui critic seat)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py agent exits 0; critic charter: adversarially grade ONE a2ui artifact (payload | catalog row | corpus record) against the NAMED .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-{payload,catalog,corpus}.md rubric, returning severity-classified file:line-cited findings, per-dimension scores, and — when judging corpus records — the ADR-0068 VerdictsFile JSON shape citing the rubric's version: marker; never edits source; tools Read/Grep/Glob/Bash; generator≠critic asserted (no maker verbs in description); fences the component-reviewer (ui-* controls) and doc-reviewer (prose docs) siblings; agent-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: tools/harness/validate-payload.ts (the loop's deterministic instrument)
nodes.accept: node --experimental-strip-types packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/harness/validate-payload.ts <payload.json> [--catalog agent-ui] exits 0 printing {ok:true, repairs:[...]} on a known-good seed payload (healed form noted when heal changed it) and exits 1 printing structured {code, path, message} failures on planted-invalid fixtures (unknown component · dangling child · malformed pointer · wrong version pin — one fixture each, checked in a slice-report run log); composes heal + validateA2ui + the default catalog via relative imports of the pure core (ADR-0062: the core computes, the shell does IO; the catalog JSON-import Node workaround mirrors import-seeds.ts); zero new deps; npm run check green

nodes.label: judge machinery + the quarantine contract: src/corpus/judge.ts (pure verdict adapter) · store.ts includeQuarantined · corpus-data.test.ts AMENDMENT (B1) · tools/corpus/rescore.ts · import-seeds --verdicts/--replace + quarantined-name halt
nodes.accept: npm run check green and npm test green with src/corpus/judge.test.ts: parseVerdictsFile(text, expectedRubricVersion) validates the ADR-0068 shape (rubric id + rubricVersion matching the CALLER-supplied expected version — the Node shell reads a2ui-corpus.md's version: marker, the pure core never touches the fs [ADR-0062] — + judgedBy + per-name {qualityScore, passed, failingDimensions?}) rejecting malformed input with structured errors; createVerdictJudge(file) returns a Judge (admit.ts:48 interface) whose score() maps record.name -> verdict; a name ABSENT from the file makes score() throw the named unjudged-candidate error (fail-closed) and import-seeds surfaces it as report+halt (the theta_dup precedent); the corpus barrel (src/corpus/index.ts) exports the adapter (TEN core modules; purity greps stay green). STORE: all() gains includeQuarantined?: boolean (default false — every existing caller's consumption semantics unchanged, tested). GATE AMENDMENT (ADR-0068 cl.6, B1): corpus-data.test.ts's never-quarantined assertion (:45) REMOVED and its over-broad 'invariant ii' cite corrected to facet-only — quarantined lines are legal (parse + validateRecord + facet assert for ALL lines; tier-1 + hash-recompute legs run for NON-quarantined lines only); a planted quarantined line passes, a planted quarantined line with a schema defect still fails (negative control). RESCORE (Node shell): whole verdicts file validated + ALL updates computed BEFORE one serialize (all-or-nothing, proven by a planted mid-file failure writing NOTHING); applies ONLY to records with absent qualityScore — at/above bar writes meta.qualityScore, below bar sets status:'quarantined' (ONE-WAY under rescore); unknown name → halt; conflicting re-verdict → halt; identical verdict → no-op (second identical run is a byte-level no-op); records NOT named in the verdicts file are untouched and reported still-unjudged (the inverse clause — rescore is deliberately partial). IMPORT GUARDS (M1): warmDedupIndex enumerates with includeQuarantined:true (re-importing an identical seed against a quarantined record yields E_DUP, tested); a candidate that clears dedup whose name matches a stored QUARANTINED record HALTS with nothing written; import-seeds --replace <name> re-admits through the FULL judged pipeline with the replaced record's exact+near dedup signatures OMITTED from warming for that run (the self-collision rule — an improved seed is near-identical to its predecessor BY CONSTRUCTION; the test fixture MUST be a near-identical improved variant of the quarantined seed, not a dissimilar payload), logging prior status + canonicalHash in the run report, status recomputed by admission

nodes.label: activation: back-score the 11 phase-1 records + prove E_QUALITY end-to-end
nodes.accept: a2ui-reviewer grades all 11 committed shard records against a2ui-corpus.md producing ONE verdicts file citing the rubric's version: marker (its repo home named in the slice report); rescore run updates corpus/exemplar/v1_0/agent-ui.jsonl: every record carries meta.qualityScore or is status:'quarantined' with the failing dims in the run report; the AMENDED corpus-data.test.ts (h11) is green either way; git diff over the shard shows only qualityScore/status fields changed (byte-stable otherwise); the E_QUALITY negative control is a PLANTED below-bar candidate authored as a TEMPORARY shelf seed (touches src/examples/<module>.ts + index.ts + SEEDS_BY_MODULE), whose import-seeds --verdicts run exits 1 with E_QUALITY + failingDimensions and writes NOTHING — then the plant is REVERTED and the working tree shown clean (both facts in the run log; corpus SPEC-R8 AC2 falsifiable at last — ADR-0060's named trigger honored); the 'absent qualityScore' marker count goes 11 -> 0 (or the quarantine delta is itemized)

nodes.label: routing-trigger corpora + routing_eval tripwire for the two new skills
nodes.accept: each new skill ships scripts/routing-corpus.json (>= 8 positives incl. >= 2 paraphrases, >= 6 negatives drawn from sibling-skill territory: component-author, a2ui-builder dispatch phrasings, docs-author); python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-author/scripts/routing_eval.py <desc> <corpus> --min-f1 0.7 exits 0 for both; the named misses/grabs are READ and dispositioned in the slice report (the aid is a tripwire, not certification — the SPEC-N2 v0.2 reading); harness_checks description-lint arms stay green

nodes.label: scripts/harness_wiring_check.py (governance + reachability, manual gate)
nodes.accept: python3 scripts/harness_wiring_check.py exits 0: runs harness_checks.py (skill|agent|rubric mode) over the ENUMERATED harness artifact set (SPEC §5.1 — a2ui-builder deliberately OUT of the maker→graded-by enumeration, stated in the script's enumeration comment: it is graded by SPEC/LLD acceptance rows + wave reviewer seats, not a harness rubric) AND asserts reachability — every maker agent's 'graded by' rubric file exists; every skill's referenced tools/gates/rubrics resolve to real paths; every a2ui-*.md rubric is referenced by >= 1 agent or skill (no orphan — SPEC-R5 AC1); a planted dangling rubric reference AND a planted self-grading maker line each make it exit 1 (negative controls, run and shown in the slice report); stdlib-only python; wired as a MANUAL gate (the npm-run-size precedent, ADR-0040 §3) with the promotion trigger recorded in the LLD

nodes.label: reference artifact: the compose→verify loop proven end-to-end (SPEC-N4)
nodes.accept: a2ui-composer, dispatched with a realistic intent and corpus conditioning, produces a payload that (a) passes validate-payload with zero failures and (b) is graded >= 4/5 on every gated a2ui-payload dimension by a2ui-reviewer in a fresh context, within <= 3 HOST-orchestrated compose->verify rounds (the SPEC-R6 bound; harness LLD §6 — gate failures feed back to the composer freely, and a below-bar critic verdict returns to it VERBATIM as the next round's input); the round count, gate outputs, and every verdict recorded by the host in the slice report (SPEC-N4 evidence); the composer never SELF-GRADES (it assigns no rubric scores to its own output — SPEC-R8); the artifact's home is named in the report (candidate seed for a future shelf addition or a committed test fixture — admission NOT required this wave)

actions.label: reconcile the harness docs to the realized corpus-store estate (honest realized/still-needed/obsolete boundary)
pin the right-sizing and judge-mechanism decisions as ratifiable ADRs
define what a good A2UI payload is (the referential standard judgment grades against)
define what a good catalog row is
define what a corpus-admissible record is (the SPEC-R8 tier-2 bar, aggregation + version included)
teach payload composition as a recurring procedure (patterns + composition + the bounded loop)
teach corpus curation as a thin procedure over the realized pipeline
make payloads corpus-conditioned and mechanically self-checked (the maker seat)
grade a2ui artifacts against the rubrics in a fresh context (the critic seat)
let any seat validate a payload deterministically from the CLI (the loop's floor)
adapt critic verdicts into the admission judge seam with quarantine survivable end-to-end (E_QUALITY activation, fail-closed on the unjudged, gate amended, imports guarded)
back-score the phase-1 unjudged records honestly (score or quarantine, never silent)
trip-wire the skills' routing surface mechanically
keep the harness wiring reachable and governed by a script, not judgment
prove the whole loop on one reference artifact (generation -> gates -> critic, bounded, host-orchestrated)

edges.why: the rubric's existence/home/gate-role encodes ADR-0067's reshape — authoring before ratification pre-empts the host (corpus-wave n2 precedent)
encodes ADR-0067 (which rubrics exist, where they live)
encodes ADR-0067 (rubric home) AND ADR-0068 (the aggregation + version marker the Judge reads)
the skill set (2 not 4, jsonl-mcp deferred) is ADR-0067's ruling
the thin-pointer shape over the realized pipeline is ADR-0067's ruling
the composer/builder routing boundary is ADR-0067's ruling
the critic-seat unit (reviewer agent, not a curator agent) is ADR-0067's ruling
the CLI-as-loop-floor (procedural loop, no programmatic driver) is ADR-0067's ruling
judge.ts/rescore.ts/the gate amendment/the import guards encode ADR-0068's contract (cl.2/4/5/6)
the composer's frontmatter names a2ui-payload as its grading rubric — the file must exist to reference
the composer's charter preloads/cites the a2ui-compose skill (the procedure it executes)
the reviewer cites the payload rubric it grades with
the reviewer cites the catalog rubric it grades with
the reviewer cites the corpus rubric + emits the verdicts shape (incl. its version marker) its judging role rides
back-scoring grades against the ratified corpus rubric (the standard precedes the verdicts)
the critic seat that authors the 11 verdicts is the h9 agent
rescore/import --verdicts + the AMENDED standing gate are the machinery the back-score run executes and is judged green against
the routing corpus rides the shipped skill description it evaluates
the routing corpus rides the shipped skill description it evaluates
the wiring check's green run enumerates the shipped rubric files
the wiring check's green run enumerates the shipped rubric files
the wiring check's green run enumerates the shipped rubric files
the wiring check resolves the skill's referenced tools/rubrics
the wiring check resolves the skill's referenced tools/rubrics
the wiring check asserts the maker's graded-by edge resolves
the wiring check asserts the critic cites resolvable rubrics
the reference artifact is composed by the h8 maker seat
the reference artifact is graded by the h9 critic seat
the loop's deterministic check step is the h10 CLI
